original_abstract: In the current landscape of rapid digitalization and global connectivity, the Christian church faces an increasingly complex array of theological challenges sparked by the proliferation of contemporary heresies. These modern heterodoxies often distort biblical truths to fit secular trends, threatening the spiritual foundation of individual believers and the ecclesiastical harmony of the Body of Christ. This study investigates the nature of these contemporary religious deviations and evaluates the critical role of the church in addressing them. Employing a qualitative descriptive methodology underpinned by a comprehensive literature review, the research highlights how digital platforms have accelerated the spread of misleading doctrines, ranging from progressive theological shifts to materialistic interpretations of faith. The findings suggest that effectively countering these influences requires the church to reassert its role as a spiritual guardian through robust theological education and the maintenance of orthodox doctrinal integrity. Furthermore, the paper emphasizes the necessity for church leaders to possess strong hermeneutical qualifications to discern and dismantle false teachings. Ultimately, the study concludes that by actualizing its educational and apologetic functions, the church can safeguard the purity of the Christian faith and maintain unity amidst a landscape of diverse spiritual realities.
humanized_abstract: A rapidly-changing digital world and an increasingly interconnected society have brought about an unprecedented number of theological challenges in the context of the Christian church, which is faced with a multitude of modern heresies. Contemporary heresies result in a distortion of the biblical truths in line with the spirit of the age, which ends up affecting the faith of the individuals within the Body of Christ, and in this way it disrupts ecclesiastical unity. In this study, we will examine the character of contemporary heresies within the context of the Christian church, and the role that this institution bears in relation to them. On the basis of qualitative descriptive research method, and with the support of relevant literature, it is illustrated and verified that contemporary heresies—whether they be on the level of theological progressivism, or the reduction of the biblical faith to a materialistic level—are rapidly spread by digital media. In this context, the Christian church must acknowledge its role as a custodian of doctrine, and achieve the pedagogical and apologetic work necessary to preserve the purity of the faith, and in this way preserve the unity of the Body of Christ, in the presence of which is necessary in the context of plurality of religious realities.
